free_text: Activation cross sections of proton induced nuclear
reactions on iridium
Main reference.
Preliminary data.
50 microns natural Ir foils from Goodfellow
Standard stacked foil irradiation method was used, Al
and Cu foils served as energy degraders and beam
monitors. Four irradiations were done with low
intensity external beams of the cyclotrons, each for
half an hour, initial energies: 70 (twice), 50 and
36 MeV, beam currents: 135 and 175 nA.
Gamma spectra were measured non-destructively on a
HPGe detector.
Decay data were taken from WWW Table of Radioactive
Isotopes by Ekstrom and Firestone.
Uncertainties of the beams (0.4-2.2 MeV) were
estimated combining the uncetainties of the primary
energy of the beam, the energy degradation and the
energy straggling.
Uncertainties of the cross sections were
estimated in the knowledge of relative uncertainties:
number of the bombarding particles (7%), number of the
target nuclei (3%), decay data (3%), detector
efficiency (5%), peak area (1-10%).
